name: Pepper-Coated Roast Beef with Red Pepper-Basil Butter
recipeIngredient: 1 4lb eye of round beef roast, brought to room temperature by resting for 1 hour
1 7 oz jar roasted red peppers, rinsed and dried on paper towel, minced
3 tbsp minced fresh basil leaves, or 2 tsp dried basil
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. Place a wire rack in a shallow roasting pan.
3. Rub the beef roast with Dijon mustard, coating all sides evenly.
4. Sprinkle the roast with coarsely ground pepper.
5. Place the roast on the rack in the roasting pan.
6. Roast for 45-55 minutes, or until a meat thermometer reads 145°F when inserted into the center of the roast. Remove from oven and transfer to a carving board. Cover loosely with foil and let rest for about 10 minutes. (The temperature will rise approximately 5 degrees during resting.)
7. In a small bowl, beat the softened butter until fluffy using an electric mixer or wooden spoon.
8. Stir in the minced roasted red peppers, fresh basil leaves, and parsley.
9. To serve, thinly slice the meat against the grain.
10. Serve the flavored butter on the side to drizzle over the hot beef.
